name: Chiorba: Greens and Beans Soup (Spieler)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound leeks, white part only, cut into long thin strips, rinsed and dried
1/3 pound savoy cabbage or a mixture of seasonal cooking greens, thinly sliced or shredded
15 ounces canned borlotti beans, drained (or pinto beans or a red bean)
Salt and black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté the leeks and onion for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally until softened. Add carrot and garlic. Cook for an additional 2 minutes. Stir in greens, cilantro, and tomatoes. Cover and cook for another 2 minutes.
2. Stir in red harissa paste (see variations). Add broth and beans. Cook over medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the greens are tender.
3. Combine yogurt and lemon juice. Remove soup from heat and stir in the mixture. Season to taste. Serve immediately in large shallow bowls, sprinkled with paprika. Good with warm pita bread.
[Per serving: 313 calories; 2g fiber; 34g carbs; 13g fat; 15g protein]
Variations:
- Low-fat: Use less or no oil to soften onions. Switch to low-fat plain yogurt and reduce lemon juice as desired.
- Substitute carrot with parsnip, sweet potato, or pumpkin squash.
- Tested with red kidney beans and spinach using Herbed Harissa instead of red harissa.
Tip: If you make extra soup, do not add yogurt to the reserve. Yogurt should only be warmed by the soup, never heated.
Pantry Recipes:
- Harissa is a chili pepper sauce with many variations. See recipes: Harissa: Red Chile Paste (BBC) and Harissa with Cilantro and Mint.
